featureName: Lead Magnet — Calculateur d'Indexation de Loyer
description: Calculateur gratuit d'indexation de loyer sur la landing page SEIDO. Calcul déterministe client-side couvrant 3 régions belges + corrections PEB. Capture email pour lettre type pré-remplie. Article blog SEO associé.

userStories.title: Health indices JSON data + calculation engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a JSON file with historical health indices and a pure TypeScript calculation engine, so that the UI can compute rent indexation client-side without any API call.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/indexation/health-indices.json contains monthly indices from 2010-01 to 2026-03 (base 2013=100), key format YYYY-MM
lib/indexation/calculate.ts exports calculateIndexation(input) returning { nouveauLoyer, pourcentage, formule, indiceDepart, nouvelIndice, correctionPEB }
Handles all 3 regions: Bruxelles (facteurs correctifs PEB E/F/G), Wallonie (loyer adapté phases 1+2), Flandre (CF permanent D/E/F)
Handles bail commercial (formule standard sans PEB)
Handles bail non enregistré (returns error for Wallonie, warning for Bruxelles)
lib/indexation/types.ts exports IndexationInput, IndexationResult, Region, PebLabel types
Unit tests in tests/unit/indexation/calculate.test.ts with at least 1 real-world example per region + commercial + non-enregistré cases
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: 23 unit tests passing. 4 files: types.ts, calculate.ts, index.ts, health-indices.json

userStories.title: Health indices update script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script to fetch latest health indices from Statbel and update the JSON file, so indices stay current monthly.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/update-health-indices.ts fetches data from Statbel CSV (https://statbel.fgov.be/fr/themes/prix-la-consommation/indice-sante)
Parses CSV, extracts monthly health index values, normalizes to base 2013
On success: overwrites lib/indexation/health-indices.json with merged data (existing + new)
On failure: logs error, exits with non-zero code, does NOT overwrite existing file
Can be run via: npx tsx scripts/update-health-indices.ts
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Dual-source: NBB.Stat SDMX + Statbel CSV fallback. Safe no-overwrite on failure.

userStories.title: Database migration: indexation_leads table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the indexation_leads table created in Supabase, so the API route can store lead captures.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration creates indexation_leads table with columns: id (uuid PK), email (text not null), type (text CHECK in lettre_indexation/rapport_portfolio), metadata (jsonb), source (text default landing_indexation), consent_given (boolean not null default false), ip_address (text), created_at (timestamptz), expires_at (timestamptz default now() + 24 months)
Index on expires_at for cleanup queries
No RLS policies (service role access only)
npm run supabase:types regenerates database.types.ts successfully
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Migration 20260326220000. supabase:types to regenerate after db push.

userStories.title: API route POST /api/lead-magnet with Zod + rate limiting + honeypot
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to submit my email to receive the indexation letter, so that I get a pre-filled PDF via email.
acceptanceCriteria: app/api/lead-magnet/route.ts handles POST requests
Zod schema validates: email, type enum, calcul object (loyer/region/peb/nouveauLoyer/pourcentage/formule), honeypot (max 0 chars), consent (literal true)
Uses rateLimiters.public from lib/rate-limit.ts with getClientIdentifier()
Honeypot filled → silent 200 OK (no insert, no email)
Inserts into indexation_leads via createServiceRoleSupabaseClient()
Sends email via Resend using EMAIL_CONFIG.from
Returns 200 { success: true } on success, 400 on validation error, 429 on rate limit
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Inline HTML emails for MVP. US-005 upgrades to React Email templates.

userStories.title: Lettre d'indexation email template
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire receiving the letter, I want a professional pre-filled indexation letter as email, so I can use it directly with my tenant.
acceptanceCriteria: emails/templates/lead-magnet/indexation-letter.tsx React email template with EmailLayout pattern
Template includes: owner/manager placeholder fields, tenant address placeholder, calculated new rent, formula breakdown, applicable legal reference, effective date
Props interface IndexationLetterEmailProps added to emails/utils/types.ts
emailService method or inline Resend call sends the email with the template
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: React Email template with pre-filled letter. Route updated to use renderEmail().

userStories.title: Landing page indexation calculator section
userStories.description: As a visitor on the SEIDO landing page, I want to use the rent indexation calculator, so I can instantly see my new indexed rent with full explanation.
acceptanceCriteria: components/landing/sections/indexation-section.tsx is a 'use client' component
Form includes all fields from design: bail type, region, PEB, loyer, date signature, date début, bail non enregistré checkbox
PEB options adapt to region (Flandre: A+ to F, others: A to G + Inconnu)
PEB hidden when bail commercial selected
Bail non enregistré hidden when bail commercial selected
Result displays: nouveau loyer, pourcentage, formule breakdown
Accordion 'Comment ce calcul est-il effectué ?' with 5 sections (formule, indices, correction PEB, calcul détaillé, base légale)
Content adapts dynamically to inputs (PEB warnings, region-specific explanations)
Disclaimer text displayed under results
Two CTA cards under result: lettre d'indexation (email only) + portfolio (email + nombre biens)
RGPD checkbox with link to /confidentialite required before submit
Honeypot invisible field included in email capture form
Calls POST /api/lead-magnet on CTA submit
Imported in components/landing/landing-page.tsx with dynamic import
Section has id='indexation' for anchor scroll from hero CTA
Mobile-responsive layout
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: ~470 lines. Dynamic import in landing-page.tsx. 8 apostrophe escapes fixed for lint.

userStories.title: Hero CTA button + JSON-LD FAQ schema
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a CTA button in the hero that scrolls to the calculator, and structured data for SEO.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section in landing-page.tsx has a secondary CTA button 'Calculer mon indexation' that smooth-scrolls to #indexation
components/seo/json-ld.tsx extended with FAQPage schema for indexation-related questions
FAQ questions added to data/faq.ts (6 indexation questions from design doc)
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: 6 indexation FAQs added (ids 9-14). Hero CTA already done. FAQPage JSON-LD auto-included via existing faq array mapping in app/page.tsx.

userStories.title: Blog SEO article: indexation loyer Belgique
userStories.description: As a visitor searching for rent indexation info, I want a comprehensive blog article that explains the rules and links to the calculator.
acceptanceCriteria: blog/articles/calculer-indexation-loyer-belgique.md with ~1500 words
Frontmatter: title, description, date, slug, category, tags, hub/cluster metadata
H1 includes year (2026), H2/H3 structure per design doc
CTA links to /#indexation in intro and closing
Covers: formula, regions, PEB impact, commercial leases, non-registered leases, FAQ
Article accessible at /blog/calculer-indexation-loyer-belgique
npm run lint passes (if applicable to md)
userStories.notes: 1532 words. Hub: essentiel-immo-mars-2026. 6 FAQ sections, 2 CTA links to /#indexation.
